title: Bumble Inc (BMBL) Q1 2024 Earnings Call Highlights: Strong Revenue Growth and Strategic Initiatives
text: Bumble Inc reported solid growth in Q1 2024, with total revenue reaching $268 million, representing a 10% year-over-year increase. The Bumble app specifically generated $216 million in revenue (up 11%), while Badoo and other segments contributed $52 million (up 7%). The company achieved an adjusted EBITDA of $74 million, equating to a 28% margin that exceeded guidance expectations. Net earnings improved significantly to $34 million compared to a $2 million loss in the prior year period. Total paying users increased to 4 million, up 16% year-over-year. However, Average Revenue Per Paying User (ARPPU) declined 4% overall, with the Bumble app specifically seeing a 6% decrease to $26.34, primarily due to geographic mix changes. Bumble's paying user base grew 18% to 2.7 million users. The company returned $84 million to shareholders through its stock repurchase program, demonstrating commitment to shareholder value. CEO Lidiane Jones emphasized the company's relaunch focusing on user empowerment and safety features. The platform introduced a Premium+ subscription tier and plans to integrate generative AI throughout 2024. Despite positive momentum, the company acknowledged slower top of funnel trends in the U.S. market, particularly among younger users, and flagged unfavorable foreign exchange headwinds expected to impact revenue by at least 50 basis points.
